report_name: Massive 'Megalodon' Supply Chain Attack Compromises 5,500+ GitHub Repos to Steal Cloud Credentials

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: Threat actor attributed to the 'Megalodon' and 'Mini Shai-Hulud' supply chain attack campaigns, targeting GitHub repositories and package ecosystems (npm, PyPI) to steal cloud credentials and other secrets from CI/CD environments.
threat_actors.goals: Mass theft of sensitive credentials and secrets (AWS, GCP, Azure, API tokens, SSH keys) from CI/CD environments.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack was compromising GitHub repositories and injecting malicious GitHub Actions, a key development tool in the software supply chain.

attack_patterns.name: Default Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely gained initial access to the GitHub repositories by using compromised developer credentials or tokens, which may have included default credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The primary objective of the Megalodon attack was to steal cloud credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure), API tokens, and SSH keys stored as secrets or environment variables within the GitHub Actions CI/CD environment.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The malicious GitHub Action workflow automatically collected secrets and environment variables from the CI/CD environment for exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job
attack_patterns.description: The malicious GitHub Action workflow was configured to run automatically on 'push' and 'pull_request' events, acting as a scheduled job to execute the attacker's code within the trusted context of the repository's runner.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The malicious workflow exfiltrated the stolen credentials and secrets using tools like curl or wget to an external attacker-controlled domain.

malware.name: Malicious GitHub Action Workflow
malware.description: Malicious GitHub Action workflows injected into repositories by the Megalodon campaign. These workflows were configured to trigger on 'push' and 'pull_request' events to exfiltrate secrets, environment variables, and install dormant backdoors.

tools.name: base64
tools.description: Used within the malicious GitHub Action workflow to obfuscate exfiltrated environment variables and secrets before exfiltration.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: Used within the malicious GitHub Action workflow to exfiltrate stolen credentials and secrets to an attacker-controlled domain.

tools.name: wget
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool used alongside curl within the malicious GitHub Action workflow to exfiltrate stolen credentials and secrets.

campaigns.name: Megalodon
campaigns.description: A large-scale supply chain attack campaign that compromised over 5,500 GitHub repositories by injecting malicious GitHub Action workflows to exfiltrate cloud credentials and other secrets. This was the second wave of a broader campaign attributed to TeamPCP.
campaigns.objective: Exfiltrate secrets from CI/CD environments of over 5,500 GitHub repositories.

campaigns.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
campaigns.description: The first wave of a supply chain attack campaign, preceding 'Megalodon'. It was a self-propagating worm that compromised 172 packages on npm and PyPI.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The code hosting platform where over 5,500 repositories were compromised by the Megalodon supply chain attack.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: Package registry for JavaScript, targeted by the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' worm, the first wave of the campaign.

identities.name: PyPI
identities.description: The Python Package Index, targeted by the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' worm, the first wave of the campaign.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services
identities.description: Cloud provider whose credentials were a primary target for exfiltration in the Megalodon attack.

identities.name: Google Cloud Platform
identities.description: Cloud provider whose access tokens were a primary target for exfiltration in the Megalodon attack.

identities.name: Microsoft Azure
identities.description: Cloud provider whose credentials were a primary target for exfiltration in the Megalodon attack.

infrastructure.name: Megalodon Exfiltration Endpoint
infrastructure.description: An external, non-standard domain used as an endpoint for exfiltrating stolen credentials via curl or wget from compromised GitHub Action workflows.

indicators.name: Suspicious GitHub Workflow Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious GitHub Workflow Modification
id: 2b8c1f9a-3d7e-4b0f-8c6a-9e2d1f8a7b3c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for unexpected or unauthorized commits that add or modify files in the .github/workflows/ directory.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/.github/workflows/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modifications to GitHub workflow files, which was the primary vector for the Megalodon attack.

indicators.name: GitHub Workflow Environment Variable Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: GitHub Workflow Environment Variable Exfiltration
id: 8e4d2c1b-9a0f-4d7e-8c6a-3d7e1f8a7b3c
status: experimental
description: Detects GitHub Action workflow steps that pipe environment variables to encoding commands like base64, a common technique to obfuscate exfiltrated data.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1119
  - attack.t1041
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains: 'env | base64'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command pattern used in the Megalodon attack to collect and obfuscate environment variables for exfiltration.

indicators.name: GitHub Workflow Data Exfiltration via cURL
indicators.pattern:
title: GitHub Workflow Data Exfiltration via cURL
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects GitHub Action workflow run commands that use curl or wget to send data to an external, non-standard domain.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1041
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_curl:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/curl'
      - '/wget'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-X POST'
      - '-d @-'
  condition: selection_curl
indicators.description: Detects the use of curl or wget with specific parameters to exfiltrate data, a pattern observed in the Megalodon attack.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: GitHub Workflow Data Exfiltration via cURL
